title: Confirm PCIe lane width and link training
description: Verify negotiated link width and speed before debugging transaction-layer traffic on a PCIe endpoint.
body: Use when a PCIe device underperforms or fails to be detected. Do not analyze transaction-layer packets before the physical link has trained to the expected width and speed. Actions: read the link status register, compare negotiated width and speed against the slot capability, and inspect for lane-reversal or polarity issues. Validation signals include a trained link at the expected lane count and a clean configuration-space read of the device ID. Risk boundary: a link that silently trains down to fewer lanes halves bandwidth, so flag any negotiated width below the slot capability for hardware review.
